LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT CFO RICK, INC.'S AND RICK BELGARDE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO DECEMBER 19, 2013. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANTS CFO RICK, INC.'S AND RICK BELGARDE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT ("FACC") DOES NOT PLEAD THE ELEMENT OF BREACH. THE FACC ALLEGES THAT THE CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP WAS TERMINATED ON DECEMBER 27, 2013 (FACC, 55-56), PRIOR TO THE DATE PERFORMANCE WAS DUE. (FACC, 37.) THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACC ALLEGES THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS MADE TRUTHFUL BUT MISLEADING STATEMENTS. THE FACC GENERALLY PLEADS THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS ARE ACCOUNTANTS WHO MISLEAD CROSS-COMPLAINANT INTO BELIEVING THEY WERE CERTIFIED PUBLIC ACCOUNTANTS ("CPAS"). THE FACC DOES NOT PLEAD THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS BEING CPAS WAS MATERIAL INFORMATION TO CROSS-DEFENDANTS. (SEE MORGAN V. AT&T WIRELESS SERVICES, INC. (2009) 177 CAL.APP.4TH 1235, 1262.) THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EXTORTION IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACC DOES NOT PLEAD A THREAT TO DO UNLAWFUL INJURY TO A PERSON OR PROPERTY. (CAL. PENAL CODE 519.) THE DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL TORT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS DUPLICATIVE OF OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION ALREADY ALLEGED IN THE FACC. THE DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE IS OVERRULED. THE FACC PLEADS THAT THE INTENTIONAL ACTS OF CROSS-DEFENDANTS INTERRUPTED ITS BUSINESS OPERATIONS. (SEE E.G. FACC 46-48.) THE DEMURRER TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACC DOES NOT ADEQUATELY PLEAD THAT THE PUBLIC WOULD BE MISLED BY CROSS-DEFENDANTS STATING THEY ARE ACCOUNTANTS OR AN ACCOUNTING FIRM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CFO RICK, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF NICK KRAEMER AND BARRETT RAFTERY.PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT CFO RICK, INC.'S MOTION IS GRANTED SUBJECT TO THE FOLLOWING: CFO RICK, INC. MAY TAKE THE DEPOSITIONS ON OR BEFORE MAY 24, 2018 CONDITIONED UPON PAYMENT OF REASONABLE EXPENSES AND COSTS TO EACH DEPONENT FOR TRAVEL TO A LOCATION AS PROVIDED BY CCP 2025.250(A). ALTERNATIVELY, THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING A DATE CERTAIN FOR THE DEPOSITIONS TO TAKE PLACE WITHIN THE JURISDICTIONS LIMITS SET FOR IN CCP 2025.250(A) BASED ON THE DATES RANGE PROVIDED BY COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS; AND SHALL MEET AND CONFER TO CONTINUE THE DEFENDANT'S/CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT TO A DATE FOLLOWING COMPLETION OF THE DEPOSITIONS. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000 ARE AWARDED TO MOVING PARTY.ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE PRO TEM: STEPHANIE KRMPOTIC, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-30-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-25-2018 Continued To Sep-19-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-24-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Atty. Philip Seltzer of the Rubenstein Law, Tel. No. 1-415-967-1970 appearing for
the Plaintiff;
Atty. Matthew J. Olson of Macdonald/Fernandez LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-362-0449
appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants and cross-complainants Nick Kraemer, Barrett Raftery and Doorman Property Management S Corp.'s motion for summary judgment or alternatively motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. There are triable disputes whether: 1) plaintiff and cross-defendant CFO Rick, Inc. performed all of the duties required of it per the parties' contract or was excused from doing so; 2) any of the services performed or required to be performed by CFO Rick per the parties' contract required CFO Rick, Rick Belgrade or the persons who performed those services to be a licensed CPA; and 3) CFO Rick or Mr. Belgrade made any misrepresentation in connection with their statements that they performed accounting services. Mr. Belgrade's declaration alone creates triable disputes on all of these issues. Apparently recognizing that, the reply repeatedly states that the declaration is "not credible" and should be disregarded, citing Estate of Housley (1997) 56 Cal. App. 4th 342, 259-60.) Housley is not helpful to defendants. That case states that "the sole declaration of a party opposing a summary judgment motion which raise a triable issue of fact is sufficient to deny that motion'.Code of Civil Procedure section 437c, subdivision (e) does not allow a trial court to weigh the credibility of a declaration submitted by the party opposing a summary judgment motion and then grant the motion, except where the declaration is facially so incredible as a matter of law that the moving party otherwise would be entitled to summary judgment." Because the face of Mr. Belgrade's declaration does not disclose anything incredible about any of the statements in that declaration, the extremely narrow and very likely almost never successfully invoked "facial incredible" exception mentioned but found inapplicable in Housley is also inapplicable here. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion To Compel Depositions Of Nick Kraemer And Barrett Raftery
Off Calendar Apr-30-2018 Advanced From May-17-18 To May-15-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion To Compel Depositions Of Nick Kraemer And Barrett Raftery
Off Calendar May-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Philip Seltzer, Esq. 415-967-1970 for Plaintiff CFO RICK, INC.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CFO RICK, INC.'s Motion To Compel Depositions Of Nick Kraemer And Barrett Raftery.

Plaintiff/Cross-defendant CFO Rick, Inc.'s motion is granted subject to the following: CFO Rick, Inc. may take the depositions on or before May 24, 2018 conditioned upon payment of reasonable expenses and costs to each deponent for travel to a location as provided by CCP 2025.250(a). Alternatively, the parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding a date certain for the depositions to take place within the jurisdictions limits set for in CCP 2025.250(a) based on the dates range provided by counsel for Defendant/Cross-complainants; and shall meet and confer to continue the Defendant's/Cross-complainant's Motion for Summary Judgment to a date following completion of the depositions. Sanctions in the amount of $2,000 are awarded to moving party.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Stephanie Krmpotic, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-31-2013 Continued To Mar-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KYMBERLEIGH KORPUS, ESQ., 212.203.5197, FOR CROSS DEFENDANTS SFO RICK, INC. AND RICK BELGARDE.
GREGORY BRYAN, ESQ., 415.674.1235, FOR CROSS COMPLAINANTS NICK KRAEMER, BARRETT RAFTERY AND DOURMAN PROPERTY MANAGEMENT.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANTS CFO RICK, INC.'S and RICK BELGARDE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS Sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") does not plead the element of breach. The FACC alleges that the contractual relationship was terminated on December 27, 2013 (FACC, 55-56), prior to the date performance was due. (FACC, 37.) The demurrer to the second cause of action for fraud is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The FACC alleges that Cross-Defendants made truthful but misleading statements. The FACC generally pleads that Cross-Defendants are accountants who mislead Cross-Complainant into believing they were Certified Public Accountants ("CPAs"). The FACC does not plead that Cross-Defendants being CPAs was material information to Cross-Defendants. (See Morgan v. AT&T Wireless Services, Inc. (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1235, 1262.) The demurrer to the third cause of action for extortion is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The FACC does not plead a threat to do unlawful injury to a person or property. (Cal. Penal Code 519.) The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for intentional tort is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action is duplicative of other causes of action already alleged in the FACC. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage is overruled. The FACC pleads that the intentional acts of Cross-Defendants interrupted its business operations. (See e.g. FACC 46-48.) The demurrer to the ninth cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The FACC does not adequately plead that the public would be misled by Cross-Defendants stating they are accountants or an accounting firm. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
